perturbation.original_text: In addition to the foregoing, the term “Evaluation Material” also shall be deemed to include (i) the fact that any Evaluation Material has been made available hereunder, that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a Transaction and any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto (including the status thereof), and (ii) the portion of any notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations or other documents prepared by each party or its Representatives which contain or describes the information furnished to such party or its Representatives pursuant hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: In addition to the foregoing, the term “Evaluation Material” also shall be deemed to include (i) the fact that any Evaluation Material has been made available hereunder, that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a Transaction and any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto (including the status thereof).
perturbation.explanation: By omitting '(ii) the portion of any notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations or other documents prepared by each party or its Representatives which contain or describes the information furnished to such party or its Representatives pursuant hereto,' from the definition of 'Evaluation Material,' this creates a contradiction in later sections. Now, when Section 3 refers to the use and non-disclosure of 'Evaluation Material,' it's unclear whether the restriction applies to the internal documents derived from the original furnished information. This ambiguity makes enforcement problematic.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees that it shall (and it shall cause its Representatives to) use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a Transaction and for no other purpose, and that the receiving party shall (and shall cause its Representatives to) keep the Evaluation Material confidential and not disclose any Evaluation Material to any third party; provided , however , that the receiving party may disclose any Evaluation Material (x) if the disclosing party gives its prior express written consent to this disclosure, or (y) to its Representatives who are actively or directly participating in its evaluation of a Transaction or who otherwise need to know such information for the sole purpose of evaluating a Transaction (and the receiving party shall take appropriate measures to ensure that its Representatives comply with the terms of this Agreement).
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Evaluation Material' to include both the fact of discussions and any derived internal documents. The changed text omits the part about internal documents. The contradicted text requires 'Evaluation Material' to be kept confidential. Because the changed text removes internal documents from the definition, it is now unclear whether the confidentiality obligation applies to internal documents derived from the disclosed information, creating a strong contradiction in the application and scope of the confidentiality obligation.

perturbation.original_text: (2) Purpose of Disclosure of Evaluation Material . Each party understands and agrees that it will be disclosing to the other party and/or its Representatives under this Agreement (i) information solely for the purpose of enabling the receiving party and its Representatives to evaluate a Transaction, and reasonably believes that such disclosure will not affect, in any way, the parties’ relative competitive position with respect to each other or to any third parties (including any of the parties’ affiliates), and (ii) only that information which is reasonably necessary to enable each party and its Representatives to evaluate a Transaction (and that information which is not reasonably necessary for such purpose will not be disclosed to a party or its Representatives).
perturbation.changed_text: (2) Purpose of Disclosure of Evaluation Material . Each party understands and agrees that it will be disclosing to the other party and/or its Representatives under this Agreement (i) information solely for the purpose of enabling the receiving party and its Representatives to evaluate a Transaction, and reasonably believes that such disclosure will not affect, in any way, the parties’ relative competitive position with respect to each other or to any third parties (including any of the parties’ affiliates).
perturbation.explanation: The removal of the clause stating '(ii) only that information which is reasonably necessary to enable each party and its Representatives to evaluate a Transaction (and that information which is not reasonably necessary for such purpose will not be disclosed to a party or its Representatives)' creates an in-text contradiction. Now, the agreement lacks a clear limitation on the scope of disclosure. While the purpose is stated, there's no longer an explicit prohibition against disclosing information deemed 'not reasonably necessary.' This contradicts the overall intent of confidentiality and raises concerns about over-disclosure, making specific enforcement harder.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees that it shall (and it shall cause its Representatives to) use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a Transaction and for no other purpose, and that the receiving party shall (and shall cause its Representatives to) keep the Evaluation Material confidential and not disclose any Evaluation Material to any third party; provided , however , that the receiving party may disclose any Evaluation Material (x) if the disclosing party gives its prior express written consent to this disclosure, or (y) to its Representatives who are actively or directly participating in its evaluation of a Transaction or who otherwise need to know such information for the sole purpose of evaluating a Transaction (and the receiving party shall take appropriate measures to ensure that its Representatives comply with the terms of this Agreement).
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that only reasonably necessary information should be disclosed. The changed text removes this limitation, creating a contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text' which states that Evaluation Material should be used 'solely for the purpose of evaluating a Transaction'. By removing the clause limiting disclosure to only necessary information, the changed text opens the door to disclosing information beyond what's necessary for evaluation, thus contradicting the provision that the material should be used 'solely' for evaluation purposes. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition, where removing essential language creates a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (4) Required Disclosure . In the event that a party or any of its Representatives are requested pursuant to, or required by, applicable law, regulation (including, without limitation, any rule or regulation of any national securities exchange or stock market on which any of the receiving party’s securities are listed or quoted) or legal process to disclose any of the Evaluation Material, the party requested or required to make the disclosure shall, unless legally prohibited from doing so, provide the other party with prompt notice of such request or requirement in order to enable the other party to (i) seek an appropriate protective order or other remedy, (ii) consult with the party requested or required to make the disclosure and its Representatives with respect to taking steps to resist or narrow the scope of such request or legal process (and such party shall reasonably cooperate with the other party in this respect and, if such cooperation is material, at the other party’s sole expense), or (iii) to waive compliance, in whole or in part, with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: (4) Required Disclosure . In the event that a party or any of its Representatives are requested pursuant to, or required by, applicable law, regulation or legal process to disclose any of the Evaluation Material, the party requested or required to make the disclosure shall, unless legally prohibited from doing so, provide the other party with prompt notice of such request or requirement in order to enable the other party to (i) seek an appropriate protective order or other remedy, (ii) consult with the party requested or required to make the disclosure and its Representatives with respect to taking steps to resist or narrow the scope of such request or legal process (and such party shall reasonably cooperate with the other party in this respect and, if such cooperation is material, at the other party’s sole expense), or (iii) to waive compliance, in whole or in part, with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Omitting the phrase '(including, without limitation, any rule or regulation of any national securities exchange or stock market on which any of the receiving party’s securities are listed or quoted)' from Section 4 narrows the scope of what constitutes 'applicable law, regulation'. While the section still addresses required disclosures, removing this specific example makes it ambiguous whether disclosures required by securities exchange rules are covered. This creates a potential in-text contradiction if Section 11 (Securities Laws) assumes those rules are covered under Section 4, and Section 4 now implicitly excludes them. This affects the enforceability, especially if a disclosure violation occurs due to a misunderstanding of whether a securities exchange rule triggers the notification requirements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (11) Securities Laws . Each party is aware, and will advise its Representatives who are informed of the matters that are the subject of this Agreement, of the restrictions imposed by the United States securities laws on the purchase or sale of securities by any person who has received material, non-public information from the issuer of such securities and on the communication of such information to any other person when it is reasonably foreseeable that such other person is likely to purchase or sell such securities in reliance upon such information.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifically included rules and regulations of securities exchanges in the required disclosures, while the changed text omits this specific example. This omission creates ambiguity about whether disclosures required by securities exchange rules are covered under Section 4. Because Section 11 discusses securities laws, which would require disclosure, there is a potential contradiction because Section 4 no longer explicitly states that the rules and regulations of securities exchanges are included in required disclosures. The original language gave a specific example, and removing it could imply that securities exchanges are no longer included.

perturbation.original_text: (6) Termination of Discussions . All Evaluation Material shall remain the property of the disclosing party and the receiving party agrees not to assert any claim of title or ownership in the disclosing party’s Evaluation Material or any portion thereof. If either party decides that it does not wish to proceed with a Transaction, such party shall promptly inform the other party of that decision by giving a written notice of termination. At any time upon the request of either disclosing party for any reason, each receiving party and its Representatives shall promptly deliver to the disclosing party or destroy all Evaluation Material (and all copies, reproductions thereof) furnished to the receiving party or its Representatives by or on behalf of the disclosing party pursuant hereto (whether in hard-copy form or on intangible media, such as electronic mail or computer files), provided, however, that, the receiving party or its Representatives shall not, in connection with the foregoing obligations, be required to identify or delete Evaluation Material held electronically in archive or backup systems in accordance with general systems archiving and backup policies. Upon the request of the disclosing party, a duly authorized representative of the receiving party shall certify to the disclosing party that the receiving party and its Representatives have complied with this paragraph (6). Notwithstanding the return or destruction of all Evaluation Material, each party and its Representatives shall continue to be bound by its obligations of confidentiality and other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: (6) Termination of Discussions . All Evaluation Material shall remain the property of the disclosing party and the receiving party agrees not to assert any claim of title or ownership in the disclosing party’s Evaluation Material or any portion thereof. If either party decides that it does not wish to proceed with a Transaction, such party shall promptly inform the other party of that decision by giving a written notice of termination. At any time upon the request of either disclosing party for any reason, each receiving party and its Representatives shall promptly deliver to the disclosing party or destroy all Evaluation Material (and all copies, reproductions thereof) furnished to the receiving party or its Representatives by or on behalf of the disclosing party pursuant hereto (whether in hard-copy form or on intangible media, such as electronic mail or computer files). Upon the request of the disclosing party, a duly authorized representative of the receiving party shall certify to the disclosing party that the receiving party and its Representatives have complied with this paragraph (6). Notwithstanding the return or destruction of all Evaluation Material, each party and its Representatives shall continue to be bound by its obligations of confidentiality and other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of the clause 'provided, however, that, the receiving party or its Representatives shall not, in connection with the foregoing obligations, be required to identify or delete Evaluation Material held electronically in archive or backup systems in accordance with general systems archiving and backup policies' from Section 6 introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the scope of the obligation to return or destroy Evaluation Material. The original agreement acknowledged the practical difficulties of deleting data from backup systems, but now, without that clause, Section 6 implies a stricter, potentially impossible requirement to delete all Evaluation Material, even from archives. This creates an inconsistency with the intended practical application of the agreement. This omission results in a contradiction with the final sentence of the section, as the original clause clarifies the extent of the obligation, which is now ambiguous.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (6) Termination of Discussions . All Evaluation Material shall remain the property of the disclosing party and the receiving party agrees not to assert any claim of title or ownership in the disclosing party’s Evaluation Material or any portion thereof. If either party decides that it does not wish to proceed with a Transaction, such party shall promptly inform the other party of that decision by giving a written notice of termination. At any time upon the request of either disclosing party for any reason, each receiving party and its Representatives shall promptly deliver to the disclosing party or destroy all Evaluation Material (and all copies, reproductions thereof) furnished to the receiving party or its Representatives by or on behalf of the disclosing party pursuant hereto (whether in hard-copy form or on intangible media, such as electronic mail or computer files). Upon the request of the disclosing party, a duly authorized representative of the receiving party shall certify to the disclosing party that the receiving party and its Representatives have complied with this paragraph (6). Notwithstanding the return or destruction of all Evaluation Material, each party and its Representatives shall continue to be bound by its obligations of confidentiality and other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the clause that previously excused the receiving party from having to identify or delete Evaluation Material held electronically in archive or backup systems. This omission directly contradicts the obligation in the first part of the paragraph to 'promptly deliver to the disclosing party or destroy all Evaluation Material', because without the omitted clause, the receiving party is now obligated to delete everything, including information in archives, which is a stronger and less feasible standard that is imposed. The contradicted text is the entire Section 6.

perturbation.original_text: (9) Standstill . You agree that, for a period of twelve (12) months from the date of this Agreement, unless you receive the prior authorized approval of an authorized Isilon officer or director, you will not directly or indirectly (including, without limitation, by assisting or forming a group (a “ l3D Group ”) within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 (as amended, the “ Exchange Act ”)): (a) acquire or offer to acquire, seek, propose or agree to acquire, by means of a repurchase, tender or exchange offer, business combination or in any other manner, beneficial ownership of five percent (5%) or more of any securities or assets of Isilon (including any securities or assets of Isilon that you or any of your controlled affiliates already, directly or indirectly, own) including, without limitation, rights or options to acquire such ownership; (b) seek or propose to influence, advise, change or control the management, Board of Directors, governance or certificate of incorporation or bylaws, including, without limitation, by means of a solicitation of proxies (as such terms are defined in Rule l4a-l of Regulation l4A promulgated pursuant to Section 14 of the Exchange Act, disregarding clause (iv) of Rule l4a-l(l)(2) and including any otherwise exempt solicitation pursuant to Rule l4a-2(b)) or participating in any election contest or seeking to influence, advise or direct the vote of any holder of securities of Isilon; (c) offer, seek or propose any merger, consolidation, business combination, recapitalization, restructuring or other extraordinary transaction with respect to Isilon or any of its subsidiaries or businesses; (d) make any request to amend or waive this provision or any other provision of this paragraph (9); (e) make any public disclosure, or take any action which would reasonably be expected to require Isilon to make any public disclosure, with respect to any of the matters set forth in this Agreement; or (f) enter into any discussions (excluding discussions with your Representatives), arrangements, understanding or agreement with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing, including, without limitation, forming, joining or otherwise participating in any l3D Group in connection with any of the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: (9) Standstill . You agree that, for a period of twelve (12) months from the date of this Agreement, unless you receive the prior authorized approval of an authorized Isilon officer or director, you will not directly or indirectly: (a) acquire or offer to acquire, seek, propose or agree to acquire, by means of a repurchase, tender or exchange offer, business combination or in any other manner, beneficial ownership of five percent (5%) or more of any securities or assets of Isilon (including any securities or assets of Isilon that you or any of your controlled affiliates already, directly or indirectly, own) including, without limitation, rights or options to acquire such ownership; (b) seek or propose to influence, advise, change or control the management, Board of Directors, governance or certificate of incorporation or bylaws, including, without limitation, by means of a solicitation of proxies or participating in any election contest or seeking to influence, advise or direct the vote of any holder of securities of Isilon; (c) offer, seek or propose any merger, consolidation, business combination, recapitalization, restructuring or other extraordinary transaction with respect to Isilon or any of its subsidiaries or businesses; (d) make any request to amend or waive this provision or any other provision of this paragraph (9); (e) make any public disclosure, or take any action which would reasonably be expected to require Isilon to make any public disclosure, with respect to any of the matters set forth in this Agreement; or (f) enter into any discussions (excluding discussions with your Representatives), arrangements, understanding or agreement with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of the clause '(including, without limitation, by assisting or forming a group (a “ l3D Group ”) within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 (as amended, the “ Exchange Act ”))' and '(as such terms are defined in Rule l4a-l of Regulation l4A promulgated pursuant to Section 14 of the Exchange Act, disregarding clause (iv) of Rule l4a-l(l)(2) and including any otherwise exempt solicitation pursuant to Rule l4a-2(b))' from the beginning of Section 9 and subsection (b) respectively, creates an in-text contradiction because the specific prohibition against forming or assisting a 13D Group and also the details of solicitation of proxies are now less defined. While the standstill provision remains, the removal of the explicit reference to Exchange Act regulations and proxy rules creates ambiguity, potentially making it more difficult to enforce the standstill agreement. This is because certain activities that would have been clearly prohibited under the original language now become subject to interpretation, undermining the certainty the original explicit terms provided. This omission affects the meaning of the words 'directly or indirectly', and the solicitation of proxies in subsection (b).
perturbation.contradicted_text: (9) Standstill . You agree that, for a period of twelve (12) months from the date of this Agreement, unless you receive the prior authorized approval of an authorized Isilon officer or director, you will not directly or indirectly: (a) acquire or offer to acquire, seek, propose or agree to acquire, by means of a repurchase, tender or exchange offer, business combination or in any other manner, beneficial ownership of five percent (5%) or more of any securities or assets of Isilon (including any securities or assets of Isilon that you or any of your controlled affiliates already, directly or indirectly, own) including, without limitation, rights or options to acquire such ownership; (b) seek or propose to influence, advise, change or control the management, Board of Directors, governance or certificate of incorporation or bylaws, including, without limitation, by means of a solicitation of proxies or participating in any election contest or seeking to influence, advise or direct the vote of any holder of securities of Isilon; (c) offer, seek or propose any merger, consolidation, business combination, recapitalization, restructuring or other extraordinary transaction with respect to Isilon or any of its subsidiaries or businesses; (d) make any request to amend or waive this provision or any other provision of this paragraph (9); (e) make any public disclosure, or take any action which would reasonably be expected to require Isilon to make any public disclosure, with respect to any of the matters set forth in this Agreement; or (f) enter into any discussions (excluding discussions with your Representatives), arrangements, understanding or agreement with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits specific references to 'l3D Group' and Exchange Act regulations regarding proxy solicitations, which were present in the original text. The contradicted text still includes the general standstill provision from the Changed Text. The omission weakens the definition of prohibited activities related to influencing the company, creating ambiguity and making enforcement more challenging. The original text's specific terms are now absent, directly conflicting with the need for clear, enforceable obligations and constraints within the contradicted text. Because of this weakening, it goes against the original provision of the agreement.
